entities.name: UREA HYDROLYSIS
entities.description: The chemical process where urea is broken down into ammonia and carbon dioxide in the soil

entities.name: NITRIFICATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting ammonia to nitrite and then to nitrate

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The medium where urea hydrolysis and nitrification processes occur

entities.name: CHEMICALS
entities.description: Synthetic substances used to retard urea hydrolysis and nitrification

entities.name: NITROGEN TRANSFORMATIONS
entities.description: The various processes by which nitrogen changes form in the soil environment

entities.name: FERTILIZER NITROGEN EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The effectiveness with which plants utilize nitrogen from applied fertilizers

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The agricultural process of growing crops for harvest

entities.name: N METABOLISM
entities.description: The biochemical processes by which plants absorb and utilize nitrogen

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Biological organisms that absorb nitrogen for growth and development

entities.name: INDIGENOUS MATERIALS
entities.description: Locally sourced, cost-effective materials used to control nitrogen processes in soil

entities.name: UREASE INHIBITORS
entities.description: Specific chemicals that inhibit the enzyme urease to reduce urea hydrolysis

entities.name: AMMONIA VOLATILIZATION
entities.description: The loss of nitrogen from the soil surface into the atmosphere as ammonia gas

entities.name: LEACHING
entities.description: The downward movement of dissolved nutrients, such as urea or nitrates, through the soil profile

entities.name: DENITRIFICATION
entities.description: The microbial process of reducing nitrate to gaseous nitrogen

entities.name: NH4-N
entities.description: Nitrogen in the form of ammonium ions

entities.name: UREA
entities.description: A nitrogenous organic compound used widely as a fertilizer
